view_meta: Active connector fleet — 31 loadable modules across 7 families; registry + per-module config
purpose: MODULES — the active connector-fleet registry (configuration domain): ~31 loadable modules grouped by family with rollup health, gated ENABLED toggles, READ/MUTATE capability scope, health (live/idle/dark) with color+glyph+label, per-row 24h poll-latency sparklines (observed=territory/steel/solid vs expected budget=map/indigo/dashed), and a per-module CONFIG drawer (endpoint, secret REFERENCES, scope, schedule/rate limits, ingest normalizer mapping, gated test-connection, health-history chart). Leads with the invariant: a disabled module has NO execution path.
notes: Syntax-verified with node --check. Design decisions, in the console's language: (1) The invariant banner uses hazard tokens because "disabled = no execution path" is the safety-critical fact of this view; it carries a MUTATION OFF chip so the registry can never be read as an actuation surface. (2) Health is triple-encoded everywhere (stripe color + glyph ●/◐/✕ + lowercase label); disabled rows keep their glyph but drop to --ink4 so a deliberately-off module never reads as an alarm, while an ENABLED-but-dark module (grafana-annot) reads deviation-red. (3) Row and drawer sparklines obey the map/territory chart law: observed poll latency is steel/solid (territory), the expected latency budget is an indigo dashed line (map), missed polls are deviation-red baseline ticks; the drawer version adds a legend + min/p50/max/budget stats line (per dataviz: single observed series direct-labeled via legend, grid recessive, text in ink tokens, never series color). History is deterministic per module id (FNV-seeded LCG) so fixtures are stable across re-renders. (4) Every write-shaped control is present but gated: the ENABLED switch is a role="switch" aria-disabled state display titled "config write — gated: MUTATION OFF", and TEST CONNECTION is disabled with "design preview, no connector runtime". MUTATE-scoped rows carry a hazard GATED tag. (5) Secrets render as env:/file: reference chips with a key glyph and an explicit "values never stored/shown" hint. (6) Drawer is self-contained (scrim + aside appended to body, ESC/scrim/✕ close, focus captured and restored, aria-modal); if the host later exposes a generic openDrawer(node), modsOpenDrawer's body can be dropped into it — it does NOT reuse openSession, which is session-shaped. All animation (drawer slide, smooth scroll-to-family) is guarded by reduceMotion plus a prefers-reduced-motion media query. All names are prefixed mods/MODS_ (helpers: modsRng, modsHist, modsSparkInto, modsSpark, modsSparkLg, modsHealth, modsScopeChip, mod
